nodes.name: 麦哲伦-智利南极大区
nodes.summary: 麦哲伦-智利南极大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括智利斑䳍、Austral rail、Chilean flicker等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: Band-tailed earthcreeper
nodes.summary: Band-tailed earthcreeper（Band-tailed earthcreeper，学名 Ochetorhynchus phoenicurus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括灌丛、稀树草原，主要威胁包括人类活动扰动。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 圣克鲁斯省 (阿根廷)
nodes.summary: 圣克鲁斯省 (阿根廷)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联14种鸟类，包括Austral rail、白胁山蜂鸟、Chilean flicker等14种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 约翰·古尔德
nodes.summary: 约翰·古尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联71种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、凍原豆雁、黄腹角雉等71种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 内乌肯省
nodes.summary: 内乌肯省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联8种鸟类，包括Chilean flicker、南方鸚鵡、Great shrike-tyrant等8种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The band-tailed earthcreeper (Ochetorhynchus phoenicurus) is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Argentina and Chile.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The band-tailed earthcreeper is found in southwestern Argentina between Neuquén and Santa Cruz provinces and in the northeastern part of far southern Chile's Magallanes Region

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The band-tailed earthcreeper is found in southwestern Argentina between Neuquén and Santa Cruz provinces and in the northeastern part of far southern Chile's Magallanes Region

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The band-tailed earthcreeper is found in southwestern Argentina between Neuquén and Santa Cruz provinces and in the northeastern part of far southern Chile's Magallanes Region

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It inhabits arid scrublands and temperate grasslands

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It inhabits arid scrublands and temperate grasslands

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. It is considered to be uncommon to fairly common, and its habitat "appears to be reasonably secure, with overgrazing probably the only form of anthropogenic disturbance"
